title: CT7.N06 completed discharge record — C3 — defect account already routed
description: Concrete completed-discharge schema for CT7.N06, the certificate node “C3 — defect account already routed” of tactic CT7 (Exchange trichotomy). It refines the common node-proof record with this node's exact identity, completion state, assigned contract evidence, and any required payload or terminal certificate.

tacticId.description: Framework tactic discriminator for CT7.N06. It must be exactly `CT7`, selecting Exchange trichotomy (exchange) as the tactic that owns the certificate step “C3 — defect account already routed” in the exhaustive proof flow.

nodeId.description: Stable graph-node identifier for the obligation “C3 — defect account already routed”. It must be exactly `CT7.N06` so the evidence, dependencies, outgoing branch, and any terminal result are attributed to this step rather than another node.

nodeNumber.description: One-based ordinal of CT7.N06 within tactic CT7 (Exchange trichotomy). It must be exactly 6, matching the N06 suffix and the node's position in that tactic graph.

status.description: Required completion state for CT7.N06. It must be exactly `validated`, meaning that the node's certificate, trigger, or other validation obligation has been checked for the certificate obligation “C3 — defect account already routed”; another lifecycle value does not discharge this concrete node.

dischargedContracts.description: Contract identifiers whose proof obligations are discharged at CT7.N06. The array must contain `A-Cert`, `S-Rout`, `S-Trig`; these are precisely the contracts assigned to “C3 — defect account already routed”. Listing an identifier records discharge status but does not replace the matching typed evidence record required in `contractInstances`.

contains.description: Requires `A-Cert` to occur in CT7.N06's discharged-contract list, because this node must use the contract that validates a terminal certificate from the named witness and prerequisite contract records for the obligation “C3 — defect account already routed”.

Requires `S-Rout` to occur in CT7.N06's discharged-contract list, because this node must use the contract that packages a typed payload and verifies its unique producer-to-consumer route for the obligation “C3 — defect account already routed”.

Requires `S-Trig` to occur in CT7.N06's discharged-contract list, because this node must use the contract that checks that a payload supplies the receiving tactic's trigger, arity, interfaces, and standing hypotheses for the obligation “C3 — defect account already routed”.

contractInstances.description: Typed contract-evidence records that justify CT7.N06 from the recorded branch state. The array must contain at least one instance for every assigned schema: A-Cert (certificateClass, validationStatement, witnessRefs, requiredContractRefs, evidence); S-Rout (payloadId, producerTacticId, consumerTacticId, tupleEntries, vocabularyVerified, uniqueConsumerVerified, evidence); S-Trig (payloadId, consumerTacticId, expectedArity, componentChecks, standingHypotheses, interfaceChecks, certificateHandleChecks, evidence). Every instance must include its schema discriminator and nonempty evidence as required by the referenced contract definition; together the records must establish the displayed node statement.

schema.description: Contract discriminator for a `A-Cert` evidence instance at CT7.N06. It must be exactly `A-Cert`, identifying the contract that validates a terminal certificate from the named witness and prerequisite contract records; the same instance must supply certificateClass, validationStatement, witnessRefs, requiredContractRefs, evidence.

Contract discriminator for a `S-Rout` evidence instance at CT7.N06. It must be exactly `S-Rout`, identifying the contract that packages a typed payload and verifies its unique producer-to-consumer route; the same instance must supply payloadId, producerTacticId, consumerTacticId, tupleEntries, vocabularyVerified, uniqueConsumerVerified, evidence.

Contract discriminator for a `S-Trig` evidence instance at CT7.N06. It must be exactly `S-Trig`, identifying the contract that checks that a payload supplies the receiving tactic's trigger, arity, interfaces, and standing hypotheses; the same instance must supply payloadId, consumerTacticId, expectedArity, componentChecks, standingHypotheses, interfaceChecks, certificateHandleChecks, evidence.

certificate.description: Terminal certificate emitted when CT7.N06 closes its branch. It must be exactly `C3`, the framework's certificate for closure by an already-routed defect account; the node's `A-Cert` instance and companion contracts must validate “C3 — defect account already routed”.

statement.informal: Validate or verify: C3 — defect account already routed

x-obligation.dischargeCriteria: Provide a proof/definition/computation artifact that establishes the displayed statement from the recorded branch state and satisfies every listed contract schema.
